from source import longest_common_subsequence


def test_lcs_does_not_reuse_same_b_character():
    # When b has only one 'A', a matched character in b must be consumed (b advanced).
    # The bug keeps b unchanged after a match, so the single 'A' in b gets re-matched
    # by the second 'A' in a, yielding 'AA' — which is not a subsequence of 'A'.
    result = longest_common_subsequence('AA', 'A')
    assert len(result) == 1, (
        f"LCS of 'AA' and 'A' must have length 1 ('A'), "
        f"but got {len(result)}: {result!r} — "
        f"the same character in b was reused due to missing b[1:] advance on match"
    )
